results.rendered_html:
ni-√budh P. -bodhati (aor. -bodhiṣat,  RV. ), to learn or hear anything (acc.) from any one (gen., rarely sakāśāt), to attend or listen to (esp. Impv. nibodha, ˚dhata; rarely ˚dhasva, ˚dhadhvam);
to know, understand, consider as (often with double acc.),  RV. ;  AV. ;  Mn. ;  MBh.  &c.:
Caus. -bodhayati, to cause to know or learn, to inform, tell,  BhP.

results.rendered_html:
nibudh 1 P.
1 To know, understand, learn; nibodha sādho tava cetkutūhalaṃ  Ku. 5. 52 ; 3. 14 ;  Ms. 1. 68 ;  Y. 1. 2. 
2 To regard or consider as, deem.
3 To listen or attend to.
— Caus. To explain, inform, acquaint.
